request.question: Who was born earlier, Dallas Green or Brian Johnson?

title: Miniver Cheevy
Brian Johnson
Have It All (Bethel Music song)
Dallas Green (baseball)
Little Hell (album)
Richard B. Johnson
Dallas Green (musician)
Brian Johnson (pitcher)
Dallas Green Monarchs
Rose ave.
sentences: "Miniver Cheevy" is a narrative poem written by Edwin Arlington Robinson and first published in "The Town down the River" in 1910.

The poem, written in quatrains of iambic tetrameter for three lines, followed by a catalectic line of only three iambs, relates the story of a hopeless romantic who spends his days thinking about what might have been if only he had been born earlier in time.
Brian Francis Johnson (born 5 October 1947) is an English singer and songwriter.
In 1980, he became the third lead singer of the Australian rock band AC/DC, after the death of their second lead singer Bon Scott.
He and the rest of the band were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2003.
In March 2016, Johnson stepped down from touring on the Rock or Bust World Tour due to hearing problems.

On 20 May 2016, Johnson stated in an interview "I've had a pretty good run" in AC/DC and implied that he might not be returning by saying "I'm just thankful, really, that I came out of it in one piece."
"Have It All" is a song by Bethel Music and Brian Johnson and is Bethel Music's lead and only single from their eighth live album, "Have It All" (2016).
The live version of "Have It All" was released on February 20, 2016 available on streaming, followed by the release of the studio version on May 6, 2016.
The song was written by Brian Johnson, Bobby Strand, Lindsey Strand, Mia Fieldes, and Joel Taylor, with production being handled by Chris Greely and Bobby Strand.
George Dallas Green (August 4, 1934 – March 22, 2017) was an American pitcher, manager and executive in Major League Baseball.
After playing for the Philadelphia Phillies, Washington Senators, and New York Mets from 1960 through 1967, he went on to manage the Phillies, New York Yankees, and Mets.

Green managed the Phillies when they won their first World Series title in 1980 over the Kansas City Royals, and as general manager of the Chicago Cubs from 1981 to 1987 he built the club which won a division title in 1984, the Cubs' first postseason appearance in 39 years.
Green had a losing record both as a pitcher and as a manager.
Nonetheless, in 1983 he was inducted into the Delaware Sports Museum and Hall of Fame.
He achieved notoriety for his blunt manner.
Little Hell is the third album by City and Colour, released on June 7, 2011.

Besides Dallas Green who recorded the majority of the instrumentals himself, contributing musicians on the record include Daniel Romano of Attack in Black, Dylan Green and Scott Remila of Raising the Fawn, Nick Skalkos of The Miniatures, Misha Bower of Bruce Peninsula and Anna Jarvis and Jordan Mitchell of The Rest.
The album art is an illustration of Allard Schager's photograph "Fields of Gold", taken in a tulip field outside Alkmaar in North Holland.
Richard Brian Johnson is the author of the book "Abominable Firebug" (ISBN  ), which presents his account of daily life at the Lyman School for Boys.
Johnson invented the Rubber Ducky antenna while attending the Lyman School for Boys.
Johnson went on to a career as an engineer and inventor.
He also created the JMODEM file transfer protocol.
Mr. Johnson founded the Danvers, Massachusetts, software company, Route 495 Software, LLC, in early 2009.
Johnson is also an activist for civil rights issues, and has communicated with the President of the United States on issues involving general aviation.
Johnson has continued to give talks to groups interested in learning about the nation's first reform school.
Dallas Michael John Albert Green (born September 29, 1980) is a Canadian musician and singer-songwriter who records under the alias City and Colour.
He plays melodic acoustic and folk music and is often accompanied by a rotating number of Canadian indie rock musicians, such as Spencer Burton.
He is also known for his contributions as the lead singer, rhythm guitarist, and songwriter for the post-hardcore band Alexisonfire.
In 2005, he debuted his first full-length album, "Sometimes", which achieved platinum certification in 2006.
City and Colour began performing in small intimate venues between Alexisonfire tours.
The name "City and Colour" comes from his own name: "Dallas", a city, and "Green", a colour.
His reasoning for the name was that he felt queasy "putting the album out under the name Dallas Green".
Christopher Brian Johnson (born December 7, 1990) is an American professional baseball pitcher for the Boston Red Sox of Major League Baseball (MLB).

Johnson was part of the No. 1 recruiting class by Baseball America at the University of Florida and was named a freshman All-American by "Baseball America", Yahoo! Sports, National Collegiate Baseball Writers Association, and Louisville Slugger in 2010.
Johnson also played for the collegiate national Team USA baseball squad in the summer of 2010.
Johnson was one of only two freshmen in the entire country, the other being Gator teammate Nolan Fontana, to play for Team USA.
The Dallas Green Monarchs were a semipro baseball team in the Negro Leagues from 1940-1947 and again in 1953.
The team was ostensibly the successor of the Dallas Black Giants which had disbanded after the 1938 season.
The 1940s team played the majority of their games at Rebel Field while the 1953 team played at Burnett Field.
From 1940-1942, their intracity "rival" was the Dallas Wonders who they played for the city tournament in 1940.
The Green Monarchs won the Dixie Negro Semipro tournament in 1947 and advanced to the National Semipro Congress.
Chicago Cubs' great Ernie Banks' was a bat boy for the Green Monarchs and his father Eddie Banks was its catcher.
Green Monarch Hank "Donkey" Thompson went on to Major League Baseball playing for the New York Giants.
The 1953 team played in the North Texas Negro Baseball League.
But the 1954 and 1955 Dallas team in that League was the Dallas Bluebirds and the Green Monarchs appear to have been dissolved.
rose ave. (also known as Rose Ave. or roseave.)
is the debut studio album by folk music duo You+Me, consisting of Dallas Green and Alecia Moore (Pink).
The album was released on October 14, 2014 through RCA and Dine Alone records.
All songs were written and produced by Pink and Green except "No Ordinary Love", a song by English band Sade.
The album debuted at number-one on the Canadian Albums Chart and at number-four on the "Billboard" 200.

candidate.summarize1.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `passages`, produce the fields `summary`.

Role and goal: Summarize the first-hop evidence so the next agent can find the missing second-hop information. Produce a single short, factual `summary` that: (1) compresses the grounded facts in the provided passages; (2) exposes the bridge entity, the specific missing fact, or the retrieval direction needed for the next hop; and (3) gives concrete evidence anchors (quoted or clearly paraphrased phrases) that a retriever can use.

Content rules:
- Keep the summary concise and fact-focused. Do not attempt to produce the final answer; describe only what the passages support and what is still needed.
- Preserve the same passage facts and phrasing as you would otherwise (you may quote or paraphrase). Do not change which facts are exposed; only condense them.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from the passages to state a bridge or to justify a next-hop direction. Do not invent facts, infer new temporal facts, or treat composition as unsupported when the necessary premises are present.
- If the passages lack a required premise, or contain conflicting identity links, explicitly state which premise or identity link is missing or ambiguous so the next agent can retrieve or disambiguate it.

Date-format rule (narrow formatting change):
- When the summary includes a date token that appears in the passages (any calendar date or parenthetical date taken from the text), render that date using the canonical form FullMonthName day, year (for example, "September 27, 2017") inside the evidence description. This normalization only changes the string form of the date token; it must not add, infer, or imply any additional temporal facts, nor be used as a reason to produce a final answer.
- Otherwise, leave all other wording and evidence presentation unchanged. Preserve quotations or clear paraphrases of the same passage text; only adjust the date token's string format.

Output contract:
- Return exactly one field `summary` containing the compressed first-hop evidence, the bridge/missing-fact signal, and any retrieval direction needed for the next hop. Do not output final answers, extra metadata, or new fields.
candidate.create_query_hop2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, produce the fields `query`.

Produce exactly one compact retrieval query string (single line) in the `query` field and nothing else. The query should be a concise keyword-style search targeted to recover the missing bridge or supporting evidence needed by downstream agents.

General behavior:
- Use the visible facts in summary_1 as anchors (album titles, artist names, dates, relation terms) and compose a focused query that is likely to retrieve passages supporting the needed relation. Prefer concrete anchors and relation keywords over full sentences.
- Do not include explanatory text, user-facing prompts, or requests-for-clarification in the `query` field. Do not invent or assert missing facts (for example, do not supply an artist name that is not present in summary_1).
- Preserve the single-query output contract: emit exactly one string and no other fields or commentary.

Special-case for missing album artist or genre (narrow semantic boundary):
- If summary_1 shows an album title but does not include the album's artist or genre, or if summary_1 explicitly signals inability to determine genre or explicitly asks for the artist/passage (phrases such as "can't determine" or "please provide the artist"), then emit exactly one compact search phrase formatted as:
  <AlbumTitle> album artist genre
  Use only the visible album title as it appears in summary_1 followed by the three keywords: album artist genre. Do not add punctuation, explanatory words, alternate tokens, or candidate artist names.
- Do not apply this special-case if summary_1 already contains the artist or genre, or if the album anchor is an ambiguous short token that requires human clarification.

Fallback and downstream behavior:
- If retrieval using the emitted query returns no useful passages, the downstream agent may then ask the user for clarification or additional context. Do not ask the user inside the `query` field.
candidate.summarize2.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `context`, `passages`, produce the field `summary`.

Task: Integrate the first-hop `context` with the newly retrieved `passages` and produce a single concise `summary` that preserves the facts needed by the final answer. Resolve the earlier bridge when the supplied passages support doing so; retain uncertainty only when the post-retrieval evidence is genuinely insufficient, ambiguous, or conflicting.

Guidelines:
- Treat a concise answer in `context` as the primary claim. Do not replace that concise context answer unless the `passages` contain a direct, identity-aligned contradiction.
- Before attributing any fact from a passage to the entity or relation named in `context`, confirm identity alignment (clear aliasing or explicit linking). Do not assume two similarly named mentions are the same without such alignment.
- If the `passages` consistently corroborate the context claim and are identity-aligned, append one brief paraphrased supporting sentence that cites the corroboration (keep the summary compact).
- If the `passages` are weak, ambiguous, or conflict with each other or with the context claim (but do not provide an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction), preserve the context claim and append a single short clause that flags the uncertainty or contradiction. When flagging, you may optionally include a verbatim quoted phrase from a passage of up to 10 words to show the source wording. Do not invent resolutions beyond what the `passages` show.
- If the `passages` contain an explicit, identity-aligned contradiction to the context claim, state that the passages contradict the context claim and briefly note the conflicting passage fact (optionally with a ≤10-word quote). Only in this case may the summary stop asserting the context claim as definitive.
- Keep the `summary` compact (aim for 1–3 sentences). Use uncertainty language only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not default to uncertainty when composition across context and passages licenses a conclusion.
- You may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts from `context` and `passages` to form a licensed multi-hop conclusion; the supporting facts need not all appear in a single passage.
- Do not include long verbatim quotes, do not invent facts, and do not attempt to resolve contradictions beyond reporting them as present in the `passages`.

Output: a single `summary` string that follows the rules above.
candidate.final_answer.predict:
Given the fields `question`, `summary_1`, `summary_2`, produce the field `answer`.

Use only the information in the provided summaries as evidence. summary_2 is the later, integrated evidence state and should be the primary source for deciding the minimal answer; summary_1 may contain useful first-hop compression or retrieval-oriented language and may be used to supply premises that, together with summary_2, license a conclusion. Do not invent facts or use outside knowledge.

Emit only the minimal answer string supported by the visible summaries in the `answer` field. Do not include explanations, justification, or any additional text.

Preserve multi-hop composition: you may combine multiple explicit, identity-aligned facts across the summaries to form a licensed conclusion even when no single sentence states the whole relation. Distinguish licensed composition from unsupported inference; emit uncertainty only when a required premise, identity link, or relation is genuinely missing, ambiguous, or conflicting in the summaries.

Minimal-answer formatting rule (explicit):
- If the summaries jointly support one minimal literal and that literal appears verbatim in the summaries and consists of multiple contiguous tokens (for example: a full personal name, a full date, or a place name), emit that full literal exactly as it appears in the summaries (verbatim, unchanged). Do not truncate or coerce it to a single token.
- If the jointly supported minimal answer is itself a single-token label, emit exactly that single token (in lowercase) and nothing else.

When in doubt about whether the summaries license a single definitive literal, prefer to emit uncertainty only if the required supporting premise or identity link is actually missing, ambiguous, or conflicting. Do not make uncertainty the default when composition or transitive reasoning is required and the necessary premises are present.

Ensure the `answer` field contains only the minimal answer string supported by the summaries (subject to the formatting rule above).

response.hop1_query: Who was born earlier, Dallas Green or Brian Johnson?
hop1_docs: Brian Johnson (footballer, born 1948) | Brian Joseph Johnson (born 29 October 1948) is an English footballer, who played as a midfielder in the Football League for Tranmere Rovers.

Brian Price (American football, born 1994) | Brian Keith Price (born June 24, 1994) is an American football defensive tackle for the Dallas Cowboys of the National Football League (NFL).  He played college football at Texas-San Antonio, and was signed by the Green Bay Packers as an undrafted free agent in 2016.

Brian Johnson (footballer, born 1955) | Brian Johnson (born 21 October 1955) is an English retired footballer who played as a winger.  He made 197 appearances in the Football League for Plymouth Argyle, scoring 40 goals, and a further seven while on loan at Torquay United.

Brian Johnson (ice hockey) | Brian Johnson (born April 1, 1960 in Montreal, Quebec) is a retired Canadian professional ice hockey right winger who played one season in the National Hockey League for the Detroit Red Wings.

Brian Johnson (fullback) | Brian "Horse" Johnson (born April 6, 1979) is a former American football fullback who played five seasons with the San Jose SaberCats of the Arena Football League (AFL).  He played college football at the University of New Mexico.
Brian Rogers (baseball) | Brian Alan Rogers (born July 17, 1982 in Dallas, Texas) is a former relief pitcher in Major League Baseball who played for the Pittsburgh Pirates in 2006 and 2007.
Brian Johnson (athlete) | Brian Johnson (born March 5, 1980 in Iowa, Louisiana) is an American long jumper.
response.summary_1: Passages list several individuals named Brian Johnson with explicit birthdates: "Brian Joseph Johnson (born October 29, 1948)" (footballer) [passage 1]; "Brian Johnson (born October 21, 1955)" (footballer) [passage 3]; "Brian Johnson (born April 1, 1960 in Montreal, Quebec)" (ice hockey) [passage 4]; "Brian 'Horse' Johnson (born April 6, 1979)" (fullback) [passage 5]; and "Brian Johnson (born March 5, 1980 in Iowa, Louisiana)" (athlete) [passage 7]. None of the provided passages mention Dallas Green or give any birthdate for him. Missing bridge / next hop needed: retrieve Dallas Green's birth date (and disambiguate which Dallas Green is meant, if multiple) — and if the question intends a specific Brian Johnson not in these passages (e.g., AC/DC singer Brian Johnson), retrieve that Brian Johnson's birth date too — so the two birthdates can be compared. Use the quoted birthdate phrases above as anchors for comparison.
response.hop2_query: Dallas Green born date Brian Johnson AC/DC born date
hop2_docs: Brian Johnson (disambiguation) | Brian Johnson (born 1947) is British lead singer of Australian hard rock band AC/DC.

Brian Johnson | Brian Francis Johnson (born 5 October 1947) is an English singer and songwriter.  In 1980, he became the third lead singer of the Australian rock band AC/DC, after the death of their second lead singer Bon Scott.  He and the rest of the band were inducted into the Rock and Roll Hall of Fame in 2003.  In March 2016, Johnson stepped down from touring on the Rock or Bust World Tour due to hearing problems.  On 20 May 2016, Johnson stated in an interview "I've had a pretty good run" in AC/DC and implied that he might not be returning by saying "I'm just thankful, really, that I came out of it in one piece."
Danger (AC/DC song) | "Danger" is a single by Australian rock band AC/DC, from the album "Fly on the Wall" released in 1985.  It was written by Brian Johnson, Angus Young, and Malcolm Young.

Fly on the Wall (AC/DC album) | Fly on the Wall is an album by Australian hard rock band AC/DC.  It was the band's ninth internationally released studio album and the tenth to be released in Australia.  All songs were written by Angus Young (guitar), Malcolm Young (guitar), and Brian Johnson (vocals).  The album was re-released in 2003 as part of the "AC/DC Remasters" series.

Club Dates/Rolling Stones Tour | The AC/DC Club Dates/Rolling Stones Tour was a mini-tour done by the Australian hard rock band AC/DC and the Rolling Stones during 2003.  Both bands headlined four of the shows.

Johnson v Agnew | Johnson v Agnew [1980] AC 367 is a landmark English contract law case on the date for assessing damages.  Lord Wilberforce decided that the date appropriate is the date of breach, "or" when a contracting party could reasonably be aware of a breach.

Chris Slade | Chris Slade (born Christopher Rees, 30 October 1946) is a Welsh rock musician and drummer, best known for playing for the Australian hard rock band AC/DC.  He is the current drummer for the group, and previously drummed for the band from 1989 to 1994, performing on their 1990 album "The Razors Edge" along with their first live album with singer Brian Johnson, "AC/DC Live".  He returned to the band in February 2015 to replace Phil Rudd for the "Rock or Bust World Tour".  Slade has also played with Manfred Mann's Earth Band, Tom Jones, Toomorrow, The Firm and Asia.
response.summary_2: The earliest Brian Johnson in the provided material is Brian Francis Johnson (AC/DC), born 5 October 1947. The passages contain no birthdate for Dallas Green, so you cannot determine who was born earlier until Dallas Green's birthdate (or which Dallas Green is meant) is supplied.
response.answer: Cannot determine from the provided summaries.
